continue: Weight is trending nicely toward goal — 173.7 lb (Jun 22) down to 170.2 lb (Jul 2), a real step toward the low-160s target.
The polarized split keeps improving on the easy side — gray-zone miles fell from 38% to 32% of the last 30 days, and every run this week (just the one) was genuinely easy at HR 121.

Recovery markers are all green — HRV balanced, RHR back to 48 after the travel bump, training readiness "recovered and ready," ACWR optimal at 1.1. The body is fully absorbing load and ready for more.
change: Polarized split is 68% easy / 32% gray / 0% hard over the last 30 days — the gray zone improved, but there's been zero genuine hard/quality running in a month (the last VO2max session read as gray because its blended average HR landed at 149). This week's plan puts a real interval session back on the calendar.

Running volume has quietly dropped three weeks straight — 23.1 → 18.7 → 11.1 mi, and this past week only 4.5 mi of running (tennis filled the gap instead). Tennis is great cross-training, but it doesn't carry marathon-specific loading — next week's plan returns to ~26 mi to get back on the base-building track.

VO2max is flat at 52 (was 53 in mid-May) and the marathon prediction ticked slightly slower (3:40:49 → 3:43:03) — consistent with the lighter running volume, not a red flag, but a reason to get structured miles back on the calendar.
